subtitle: Content Strategy for Residential Electrical Lead Generation
opportunity: Homeowners searching for electrical services represent high-intent buyers with average project values of $500-5,000. Most competitors have weak content, creating opportunity to dominate local search with educational content that builds trust before the service call.

funnel.content: Educational guides, safety information, DIY vs pro comparisons
Cost guides, comparison content, project galleries
Service pages, testimonials, guarantees, financing options

audiences.name: Emergency Fixers
audiences.description: Homeowners with urgent electrical problems needing immediate help
audiences.content_needs: Fast answers, pricing transparency, availability info

audiences.name: Project Planners
audiences.description: Homeowners planning upgrades, renovations, or improvements
audiences.content_needs: Cost guides, project timelines, design inspiration

audiences.name: Safety Seekers
audiences.description: Concerned homeowners wanting inspections or peace of mind
audiences.content_needs: Safety information, warning signs, inspection details

pillars.name: Electrical Safety
pillars.topics: Warning signs, inspections, code compliance, child safety

pillars.name: Home Electrical Upgrades
pillars.topics: Panel upgrades, EV chargers, smart home, lighting

pillars.name: Cost & Planning
pillars.topics: Pricing guides, permits, timelines, hiring tips

profiles.name: Emergency Fixer
profiles.description: Homeowner experiencing an electrical problem right now. Outlets not working, breaker keeps tripping, lights flickering. Needs help fast but worried about getting ripped off by emergency pricing.
profiles.demographics: Age 30-60 | Homeowner | All income levels | Urban/Suburban
pain_points: Worried about safety risk to family
Fear of being overcharged for emergency service
Unable to diagnose if this is serious or simple
motivations: Find trustworthy electrician who won't upsell
Get fair pricing for emergency work
objections: How do I know this company is legitimate?
Will they try to sell me things I don't need?

psychographic_factors.notes: Low tolerance for safety risks, wants proven solution
Wants quick answers, not deep education
Will pay for quality emergency service
Urgent situation, no time to research
Will decide quickly once trust established

profiles.authority_hook: Response time guarantees, real-time availability, verified reviews with response times

profiles.name: Project Planner
profiles.description: Homeowner planning an electrical upgrade or renovation project. Researching costs, timelines, and finding the right contractor. Has time to make a good decision and wants to understand the process.
pain_points: Unclear what electrical work their project requires
Don't know realistic budget for electrical portion
Previous projects went over budget or timeline
motivations: Stay within budget with no surprises
Find reliable contractor they can trust
Add value to home with quality work
objections: How do I know this estimate is accurate?
What if they find more problems once they start?
Are permits really necessary for this project?
Why is there such variance in contractor quotes?

psychographic_factors.notes: Willing to invest but wants predictability
Willing to wait for right contractor
Can understand concepts but not DIY

profiles.authority_hook: Detailed cost breakdowns, project galleries with budgets, client testimonials mentioning accuracy

profiles.name: Safety Seeker
profiles.description: Homeowner concerned about electrical safety. May have older home, noticed warning signs, or just wants peace of mind. Looking for inspection or evaluation rather than immediate repair.
profiles.demographics: Age 40-65 | Homeowner 10+ years | Older home | Family with children
pain_points: Worried old wiring could cause fire
Noticed warning signs but unsure if serious
Don't know what electrical inspection involves
Unsure if concerns are legitimate or paranoia
motivations: Peace of mind about home safety
Prevent problems before they become emergencies
objections: Will inspection just be sales pitch for repairs?
How do I know inspector is qualified?
What if they find problems I can't afford to fix?

psychographic_factors.notes: Wants to understand risks and options
Has some budget but concerned about costs

profiles.authority_hook: Certifications, inspection process transparency, no-pressure evaluation approach
